title: ObservabilityAuditCompleted payload
description: Emitted by the chain per-tool-per-model-observability retrospective (T13) to record that the inference-telemetry cluster was relocated onto the read-side substrate without behavioral regression. It is the first chain of the telemetry-consolidation program (docs/TELEMETRY_CONSOLIDATION.md). Self-hosting check parallel to TelemetryAuditCompleted — the relocated inference telemetry's closing audit lands through the write-side events ledger as the proof that the consolidation preserved the cross-substrate seam. Findings are keyed to the chain completion_condition items and the refactor-discipline seven-step gate (characterization net → audit → behavior-preserving execution → parity → densification). See docs/CHAIN1_INFERENCE_TELEMETRY_DESIGN.md.

audit_doc.description: Pointer to the audit source-doc the findings were keyed against — typically docs/CHAIN1_INFERENCE_TELEMETRY_DESIGN.md + docs/TELEMETRY_CONSOLIDATION.md + the chain completion_condition items.

summary.description: One- or two-paragraph executive summary of the audit outcome — what landed, what's deferred, which downstream chains become buildable.

recommended_next_phase.description: Free-text recommendation for the next phase of work (e.g. the telemetry-consolidation Chains 2-5). Optional.

findings.description: One entry per audit checklist item. Severity uses the audit-convention enum (low / medium / high / critical); use n/a for informational findings.

item.description: Audit checklist item identifier and short description.

status.description: pass = criterion satisfied; fail = criterion not satisfied (must file follow-on); partial = satisfied with documented gap; deferred = explicitly out of scope for this chain.

evidence.description: Concrete evidence supporting status — SQL query result, test name, commit SHA, doc pointer, etc.

severity.description: Severity if status is fail/partial. n/a for informational rows.
